decisions.growth_in_period_source: first_video.posted_at — computed as (NOW - first_video.posted_at), total views from YT API
decisions.rpm_source: Gemini via PapaiAPI proxy, keyed by (niche, sub_niche, geography), cached per niche
decisions.rpm_presentation: Output the $ outcome only — DO NOT expose 'views × RPM = $' math (winner videos hide the calculation, RPM-direct mentions are loser-coded 0.75x)
decisions.phase_1_target: Clone Money Groot ('11 Hidden Faceless YouTube Niches Explained', videoId 14563, 121507 views) structurally + layer in missing winner-coded tricks (Social Blade overlay, $earnings callout, glitch SFX, faster pacing)
decisions.phase_2_target: Add proprietary slots (cohort.saturation_rank, cohort.growth_multiplier, novelty.embedding_distance, cohort.first_mover) once Phase 1 ships

data_points.label: Annual earnings claim
data_points.presentation_rule: Round to 2 sig figs ($1.1M/year, not $1,127,433/year). Use when impressive (>$50k/year).

data_points.label: Upload cadence
data_points.presentation_rule: Integer when ≥1/week ('3 videos a day', '2 videos a week'). For rare cadence use 'one every N days'. No fractions ('0.7/week' → '3 a month').

data_points.label: Daily earnings claim
data_points.presentation_rule: Use when daily $ rounds nicely ($100/day, $1k/day). If awkward ($47.83/day), pick yearly/monthly instead.

data_points.label: Views/subs gained in a known time window
data_points.db_source: (total_views_now, NOW - first_video.posted_at) → 'X views in N months'. Reserve for channels under 18mo old.
data_points.presentation_rule: Wide-open lane: only 3 corpus videos use it but 2.0× uplift. Use on ≥2 of N items per generated video for variety. Best framing: 'just N months old and already at X views'.
data_points.notes: Strongest 'this is recent and scaling' frame. Format months as integer ('11 months', not '10.7 months').

data_points.label: Monthly earnings claim
data_points.presentation_rule: Default $ slot when yearly is too small ($<50k) and daily is too odd. Most-used in corpus but the 'boring middle' — yearly/daily preferred when available.

data_points.label: Most popular video on the channel
data_points.db_source: MAX(view_count) per channel from shorts_videos; join on channel_id
data_points.presentation_rule: Always pair with the view count of that specific video. Visual direction: cut to that video's thumbnail while stating views.

data_points.label: Channel age
data_points.presentation_rule: Round phrasing: 'almost 2 years old', 'just 6 months old', '5 years ago'. The 'just N months old' framing drives the 'this is recent and replicable' narrative.

data_points.label: Earnings per video
data_points.presentation_rule: Strongest when paired with low video count ('3 videos. $70 each. That's $210 from 3 uploads.')

data_points.label: Specific niche/topic name
data_points.db_source: channel_analysis.sub_niche (preferred) or .niche; or cluster label from niche_tree_clusters
data_points.presentation_rule: Maximum specificity. 'Health' is weak; 'ASMR sleep stories' is strong. Pull the most granular tag we have.

data_points.label: Niche is saturated
data_points.db_source: niche_tree_clusters.video_count > saturation_threshold (e.g. 200) for the cluster this channel belongs to
data_points.presentation_rule: Use as 'why this matters' beat. Strongest as a contrast: 'most YouTube niches are saturated — this one isn't'.

data_points.label: Zero or low competition
data_points.db_source: niche_tree_clusters.video_count < low_competition_threshold (e.g. 20) for the cluster; also surface channel count from our index
data_points.presentation_rule: Pair with our absolute channel count: 'We track 8 channels in this niche — that's basically open water.'

data_points.label: Total lifetime views on channel
data_points.presentation_rule: Strong 'across the channel' anchor. Format readable (52M total views, not 52,418,773).

data_points.label: Tool used to make the content
data_points.db_source: Inferred from channel_analysis.content_style + is_ai_generated. Phase 1 heuristic: 'AI voiceover' → ElevenLabs; 'static images + Ken Burns' → Canva/Pictory. Phase 2 build real classifier.
data_points.presentation_rule: The 'how to start' beat. Name 1-2 tools per item. Show their logos on screen.

data_points.label: Number of videos uploaded
data_points.db_source: COUNT(*) FROM shorts_videos WHERE channel_id = X
data_points.presentation_rule: Format as integer (120 videos, not 119.6). Use 'only N' when count is small (<30) — drives 'they did this with barely any content' narrative.

data_points.label: Subscriber count
data_points.presentation_rule: Format readable (436K subscribers, 1.2M subscribers).

data_points.label: One-shot dollar figure
data_points.db_source: Same RPM math, presented as one-shot. Best for 'one viral video paid X' framings.
data_points.presentation_rule: Use when narrating top-video earnings: 'that one video has probably made around $29,000'.

data_points.label: Calendar year reference
data_points.presentation_rule: Frame the whole video as 'in [current_year]'. Use in title + opening line.

data_points.label: Specific video view count
data_points.presentation_rule: Most common claim in corpus — every item must cite at least one specific video's view count.

data_points.label: How the video is produced
data_points.presentation_rule: Universal across corpus. Always mention. Example: 'AI voiceover + animated visuals'.

data_points.label: RPM/CPM directly cited
data_points.presentation_rule: DO NOT FILL. Winners hide the calculation. Use RPM internally to compute $; output only the dollar conclusion.

data_points.label: Like count on a video
data_points.presentation_rule: DO NOT FILL. Like counts are weaker social proof than view counts. Loser-coded.

data_points.label: Time-windowed claim ('past 90 days')
data_points.presentation_rule: DO NOT FILL with 'past N days' framing — sounds hedgy. Drop the 'past' modifier: 'in 90 days' instead of 'in the past 90 days'.

data_points.label: Rank within niche cluster
data_points.db_source: RANK() OVER channel_count in same niche_tree_clusters row
data_points.presentation_rule: '4th most-active channel in this niche cluster (47 channels indexed)'

data_points.label: Growth vs cohort median
data_points.presentation_rule: '3.2× the cluster median views-per-day'

data_points.label: How geometrically isolated
data_points.presentation_rule: 'Only 3 channels in our index sit within thumbnail similarity 0.85'

data_points.label: Rank among AI-generated peers
data_points.db_source: RANK OVER channels in niche WHERE is_ai_generated = TRUE
data_points.presentation_rule: '#2 highest-performing AI-generated channel in this niche'

data_points.label: Recently-created top performer
data_points.presentation_rule: 'Created 41 days ago, already top 5% of its cluster'

data_points.label: New channels in niche per period
data_points.db_source: COUNT channels WHERE createdAt > NOW-60d AND in cluster X
data_points.presentation_rule: '28 new channels entered this niche in the past 60 days'

data_points.label: Style dominant in other niche
data_points.db_source: channel.content_style dominant in cluster A but rare in this cluster B
data_points.presentation_rule: 'This format dominates in [other niche]; only 4 channels exploit it here'

phase_1_reference_videos.title: 11 Hidden Faceless YouTube Niches Explained
phase_1_reference_videos.role: structural backbone — clone its pacing, listicle structure, and proof-first cold-open

phase_1_reference_videos.title: 20 Easy Faceless Niches Explained in 17 Minutes
phase_1_reference_videos.role: secondary reference — strongest views/subs ratio in corpus; same creator iterating

tricks_to_add_from_corpus_winners.trick: Social Blade statistics overlay during channel reveals
Whoosh SFX on cuts within a niche segment
First segment ≤2s (cold-open snappier than Money Groot's 1.42s baseline)
